chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: Next we will talk about grid gaps. i.e. whatever our lines form, our rows and columns form, we can give a gap if we want. Till now, if we have formed rows and columns, there is no gap in them. So, as soon as one item is finished, the second item starts.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: So, as soon as one item is finished, the second item starts. Or if we talk about layout, as soon as our first cell is finished, our second cell starts. There is no space in the middle. If we need space between rows and columns, then we can use the gaps.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: If we need space between rows and columns, then we can use the gaps. To bring gaps between rows, we write our row gap. And after that, we can write the value of the gap. Let's suppose we need a 10 pixels gap.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: Let's suppose we need a 10 pixels gap. So, we can write 10 pixels. And between columns, i.e. if we need a gap between two vertical tracks, then we can write its value. So, we will do the same thing here.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: So, we will do the same thing here. First of all, we take back the width and height of our items. So, our items have returned to initial sizing. Now, let's remove all the parts for now.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: Now, let's remove all the parts for now. Let's keep this as a template for our rows and columns. First of all, we will define the gap between rows. So, we can write the row gap.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: So, we can write the row gap. We will make the row gap 10 pixels. That will make it safe and refreshed. So, what happened with this?

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: So, what happened with this? Now, this highlighted purple area is showing you that this is basically my row gap. Here, my first row is the gap of 10 pixels. Then, my second row is the gap of 10 pixels.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: Then, my second row is the gap of 10 pixels. Then, my third row is the gap of 10 pixels. Then, my fourth row. Then, our fourth row comes.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: Then, our fourth row comes. We have divided it equally in four rows. So, we have four rows. And between the four rows, we have equal amount of gap.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: And between the four rows, we have equal amount of gap. Similarly, we can write our column gap. We will write column gap. And we can do this too.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: And we can do this too. We are 10 pixels. Safe and refreshed. So, what happened?

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: So, what happened? There is a gap between the columns. This was the gap of the row. This is the gap of my column.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: This is the gap of my column. This is the first column. Here, then my column gap. Then, the second column.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: Then, the second column. Then, the column gap. Then, the third column. So, we have divided it equally in three columns.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: So, we have divided it equally in three columns. And between the three columns, we have equal gap. Which is equal to 10 pixels. We can even increase the column gap.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: We can even increase the column gap. We can make it 30 pixels. Safe and refreshed. So, what happened?

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: So, what happened? This gap will show you a little bigger. As compared to the gap of rows. And column and row gap is a shorthand to write.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: And column and row gap is a shorthand to write. Shorthand means, if we write here. We can write our grid gap. In the grid gap, first we write our row gap.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: In the grid gap, first we write our row gap. Which is 10 pixels. And then, we write our column gap. Which is equal to 30 pixels.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: Which is equal to 30 pixels. Safe and refreshed. So, what happened? 10 and 30, our app is individually applied.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: 10 and 30, our app is individually applied. Along with this, if we write a single value here. So, 10 pixels will mean a single value. That means, I need a gap of 10 pixels for the row.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: That means, I need a gap of 10 pixels for the row. And for the column, I need a gap of 10 pixels. Safe and refreshed. What will happen?

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: What will happen? Now, we can see equal gap between rows and columns. If we remove the height and width for items. Then, what happened?

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: Then, what happened? That same gap is also shown between items. When this gap was not there, what were the items doing? As soon as the first item was ending.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: As soon as the first item was ending. As soon as the second was ending, the third was ending. The fourth was ending, the fifth was ending. There was no gap in between.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
chunks.text: There was no gap in between. So, if we have placed items in the grid layout. And we need a gap between items. We can create that gap in the form of grid gap.

chunks.title: 17_Grid Gaps
